name: Russian Black Bread (Ceideburg)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine rye and all-purpose flour, set aside.
2. In a large bowl, thoroughly mix the 2 1/3 cups of flour mixture, sugar, salt, cereal, caraway seed, instant coffee granules, onion powder, fennel seed, and yeast.
3. In a saucepan, combine water, vinegar, molasses, chocolate, and butter. Warm over low heat.
4. Gradually add the liquid to the dry ingredients. Beat at medium speed with an electric mixer for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Add half a cup of the flour mixture. Beat at high speed for 2 more minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
6. Stir in enough additional flour mixture to make a soft dough. Turn out on a lightly floured board and cover with the bowl. Let rest for 15 minutes.
7. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 to 15 minutes (the dough will be sticky). Place in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
8. Divide the dough into two halves, form into loaves and place in 9-by-5-inch loaf pans. Alternatively, form into two round balls and place on a greased baking sheet.
9. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until the bread sounds hollow when tapped on the top.
10. Meanwhile, combine cornstarch and 1/2 cup cold water in a small bowl. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils. Stir constantly for 1 minute. As soon as the bread is baked, brush the cornstarch mixture over the tops of the loaves. Return the bread to the oven. Bake for 2 to 3 minutes or until the glaze is set.
11. Remove from pans and cool on wire racks. Makes 2 loaves.
